title: Band Pickup
description: Calculate how many stitches to pick up for cardigan bands.
help_text:
Use this tool when you need to calculate how many stitches to pick up for a cardigan band.

Enter the gauge of the band stitch pattern and the number of rows along the sweater front edge. The tool calculates how many stitches to pick up so the band lies flat without pulling or rippling.Use this tool when you need to calculate how many stitches to pick up for a cardigan band.

What You Need
• Gauge of the band stitch pattern
• Number of rows along the sweater front edge

title: Button Bands (vertical)
description: Calculate stitch pickup and instructions for vertical button bands.
help_text:
Use this tool when adding vertical bands to the front edges of a cardigan.

Enter the number of rows along the sweater front and the gauge of the band stitch pattern. The tool calculates how many stitches to pick up and the instructions needed to knit the band so it lies flat when worn.
The tool will give you precise instructions for positioning the buttonholes using any knitting method you like.

title: Rectangle / Square
description: Calculate stitch and row counts for rectangular or square projects.
help_text:
Use this tool to create rectangular or square projects such as scarves, shawls, pillows, and blankets.

Enter the finished width and length, along with your gauge. The tool calculates the number of stitches to cast on and the number of rows needed to reach your desired size.
Use any yarn, any machine and any stitch pattern.

title: Button Placements (horizontal)
description: Calculate spacing for evenly positioned buttonholes.
help_text:
Use this tool to position buttonholes evenly along a band.

Enter the length of the band and the number of buttonholes needed. The tool calculates the spacing so the buttonholes are placed evenly from top to bottom.
The Knit it Now Buttonhole Placement Tool, will walk you through all the decisions you need to make, step-by-step and it will generate knitting instructions so you can knit bands with perfectly spaced buttonholes.

Start by entering the gauge of the stitch pattern you'll use for your band Next add the details of your buttons:
How many holes?
How long is the band?
hat size buttonhole do you need?
How much space do you want from the top and bottom edge of the band?

Armed with your answers, the tool will generate knitting instructions for you, with NO MATH.

title: Circle / Polygon Tool
description: Calculate stitch counts and shaping for knitted circles.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate knitted circles for your knitting machine.

Enter your gauge and the finished size of the circle. The tool calculates the stitch counts and shaping needed to knit a flat circle using your yarn, machine, and gauge.6 or 8 wedges
Hats
Blankets
Pillows
Shawls
... you name it!

title: Custom Sleeve
description: Generate sleeve shaping for drop or square armholes.
help_text:
You've found the perfect pattern, and sleeveless just isn't comfortable for you. With a few measurements and the Knit it Now Custom Sleeves pattern, you're the designer!

Take a sleeveless sweater pattern (with dropped shoulders) and add sleeves.

title: Dart Formula
description: Calculate short-row shaping for bust darts.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate short-row shaping for darts.

Enter the dart depth and the number of stitches involved. The tool calculates the short-row sequence needed to shape the dart and improve the fit of your sweater.
Let the Knit it Now Dart Formula Tool calculate the short-row shaping for darts.

Adding bust darts will improve the fit of your sweaters.

title: Knitters Graph Paper
description: Generate knitter’s graph paper scaled to your gauge.
help_text:
Use this tool to create full-scale knitter’s graph paper based on your stitch and row gauge.

Enter your stitch gauge and row gauge. The tool generates graph paper scaled to your gauge so you can accurately plan intarsia, motifs, or pattern shaping.Note: if you are using an IPad with Safari, you may have a problem with the download. Please use a different browser or device

title: Gauge Comparison
description: Compare gauges to see how they affect finished size.
help_text: Use this tool to see how differences in gauge affect the finished size of your knitting.

title: Gauge Calculator
description: Calculate stitch and row gauge from a swatch.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate your stitch and row gauge from a knitted swatch.

Knit a swatch, measure the stitches and rows over a known area, and enter those numbers. The tool calculates your stitch gauge and row gauge for use in patterns and planning.

title: Gauge Conversion
description: Convert stitch and row counts when using a different yarn gauge.
help_text:
Use this tool when you want to substitute a different yarn than the pattern calls for.

Enter the gauge from the original pattern and the gauge from your swatch. The tool calculates the stitch and row counts needed so your finished piece matches the pattern measurements using your yarn.Here's an easy conversion tool that can help.

title: Hood Builder
description: Generate knitting instructions for a simple hood.
help_text:
Use this tool to create a simple hood shape that can be added to a knitting pattern.

Enter your gauge and the desired hood size. The tool calculates the stitches, rows, and shaping needed to knit the hood.Choose from 4 different hood styles
Enter the dimensions of your finished neck opening and the desired hood depth.
The tool will generate a hood pattern based on your stitch and row gauge

title: Maximum Knitted Width
description: Calculate the maximum width you can knit on your machine.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate the maximum width you can knit using all needles on your machine.

Enter your stitch gauge and the number of needles available on your machine. The tool calculates the widest fabric you can knit without exceeding the needle bed.

title: Modified V-Neck
description: Generate shaping instructions for a V-neck with a flat point.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate shaping instructions for a V-neck with a flat point.

Enter your gauge along with the depth and width of the neckline. The tool calculates the decreases needed to shape the V-neck and create a flat point at the bottom.

title: Neck Bands
description: Calculate stitch pickup for neck bands.
help_text:
Use this tool to estimate the number of stitches needed for neck bands.

Enter your gauge and the dimensions of the neck opening. The tool calculates how many stitches to pick up for the band across both the front and back neck edges.

title: Pocket Placement
description: Calculate stitch counts and placement for pockets.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate the placement and knitting instructions for rectangular or square pockets.

Enter your gauge along with the finished pocket width and depth. Choose the pocket style: gathers at top and bottom, gathers at the top only, or no gathers. The tool calculates the stitch counts and instructions needed to knit and place the pocket.Top and bottom gathers: The pocket stitch count is 1.5 times "F" (the finished pocket width)
Gather at top only: The pocket is shaped with the top edge 1.5 times "F" (the finished pocket width)
No Gathers: The instructions are calculated on your "F" and "D" entry and your gauge

title: Slope Tool
description: Calculate shaping when stitches exceed available rows.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate shaping when there are more stitches than rows, such as when creating a shoulder slope.

Enter the number of stitches and rows involved in the shaping. The tool calculates how to distribute the decreases so the slope forms smoothly across the rows.

title: Stitch Multiples
description: Adjust stitch counts to fit complete stitch pattern repeats.
help_text:
Use this tool to adjust stitch counts so a stitch pattern fits correctly.

Enter the number of stitches required for the stitch pattern repeat and the number of stitches available in your piece. The tool calculates the nearest stitch count that allows complete pattern repeats.

title: Magic Formula 1
description: Calculate evenly spaced increases or decreases for shaping.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate evenly spaced increases or decreases for shaping a knitted piece.

Enter the number of stitches to increase or decrease and the number of rows available for the shaping. The tool calculates how to distribute the shaping evenly across the rows and provides the knitting instructions.Watch the video above for more details.

Use this Knit it Now Magic Formula Tool when you know the stitches and rows of the piece you are creating or altering.
Use this formula when you are working more rows than stitches.
A common use for the Magic Formula is to alter sleeve length or create waist shaping for a sweater.
 If you are working from a pattern and you know the dimensions,
use the Knit it Now Magic Formula (in/cm) Tool.

 If you are working with more stitches than rows, like a shoulder slope,
use the Knit it Now Slope Tool.

title: Magic Formula 2
description: Calculate evenly spaced increases or decreases for shaping.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate evenly spaced increases or decreases for shaping a knitted piece.

Enter the number of stitches to increase or decrease and the number of rows available for the shaping. The tool calculates how to distribute the shaping evenly and provides the knitting instructions.Watch the video above for more details.

Use this Knit it Now Magic Formula Tool when you know the dimensions of the piece you are creating or altering.
Use this formula when you are working more rows than stitches.
A common use for the Magic Formula is to alter sleeve length or create waist shaping for a sweater.
 If you are working from a pattern and you know the stitches and rows,
use the Knit it Now Magic Formula (sts/rows) Tool.

 If you are working with more stitches than rows, like a shoulder slope,
use the Knit it Now Slope Tool.

title: Triangle
description: Calculate shaping instructions for a symmetrical triangle.
help_text:
Use this tool to calculate the shaping needed to knit a symmetrical triangle.

Enter your gauge and the desired measurements for the triangle. The tool calculates the stitch counts and increase or decrease instructions needed to create the shape.

title: Yarn Estimator
description: Estimate whether you have enough yarn to finish a project.
help_text:
Use this tool to estimate whether you have enough yarn to finish your project.

Enter the gauge and the amount of knitting completed along with the yarn used so far. The tool estimates the total yarn required and helps you determine if you have enough yarn to complete the project.
A swatch, a scale and a few measurements and you'll never worry about running out again!

title: Yards Per Pound
description: Estimate whether a yarn is suitable for your machine.
help_text:
Use this tool to evaluate whether a yarn is suitable for your knitting machine.

Enter the information from the yarn label, such as weight, yardage, and recommended gauge. The tool helps you estimate whether the yarn will work well on your machine.
By using the information on the ball band, this tool will help you determine if the yarn of interest is appropriate for your machine.
